questions.question: Who were the 'Samantas'?
questions.answer: Samantas were big landlords or warrior chiefs in different regions of the subcontinent who were expected to bring gifts and provide military support to their kings.

questions.question: What title did Samantas take when they gained power and wealth?
questions.answer: When Samantas gained power and wealth, they declared themselves to be 'maha-samanta' or 'maha-mandaleshvara' (the great lord of a circle or region).

questions.question: Who was Dantidurga?
questions.answer: Dantidurga was a Rashtrakuta chief who overthrew his Chalukya overlord and performed a ritual called hiranya-garbha to become a Kshatriya.

questions.question: What does the ritual 'Hiranya-garbha' mean?
questions.answer: Hiranya-garbha literally means the 'golden womb'. It was a ritual performed to give the sacrificer a 'rebirth' as a Kshatriya, even if he was not one by birth.

questions.question: Name two Brahmanas who gave up their traditional professions to become kings.
questions.answer: Mayurasharman (Kadamba dynasty) and Harichandra (Gurjara-Pratihara dynasty) were Brahmanas who took to arms and established kingdoms.

questions.question: What high-sounding titles did the new kings often adopt?
questions.answer: They adopted titles like 'Maharaja-adhiraja' (great king, overlord of kings) and 'Tribhuvana-chakravartin' (lord of the three worlds).

questions.question: From whom did the kings collect resources?
questions.answer: Resources were obtained from producers—such as peasants, cattle-keepers, and artisans—who were often persuaded or compelled to surrender part of what they produced.

questions.question: What were the Chola inscriptions' '400 taxes'?
questions.answer: The inscriptions of the Cholas in Tamil Nadu mention more than 400 terms for different kinds of taxes.

questions.question: What was 'Vetti'?
questions.answer: Vetti was a tax mentioned in Chola inscriptions that was taken not in cash but in the form of forced labour.

questions.question: What was 'Kadamai'?
questions.answer: Kadamai was a term used for land revenue in the Chola kingdom.

questions.question: What are 'Prashastis'?
questions.answer: Prashastis are special inscriptions that contain details about rulers, often written in glowing terms to praise them. They may not be literally true.

questions.question: Who composed the Prashastis?
questions.answer: Prashastis were usually composed by learned Brahmanas, who sometimes helped in the administration of the kingdom.

questions.question: Who was Kalhana?
questions.answer: Kalhana was an author who composed a long Sanskrit poem containing the history of the kings who ruled over Kashmir in the twelfth century.

questions.question: What was the 'Tripartite Struggle'?
questions.answer: It was a long-running conflict between the Gurjara-Pratihara, Rashtrakuta, and Pala dynasties for control over the city of Kanauj.

questions.question: Why was the city of Kanauj so important?
questions.answer: Kanauj was located in the prized Ganga valley, and controlling it symbolized supreme power over the fertile region.

questions.question: Who was Sultan Mahmud of Ghazni?
questions.answer: He was a ruler from Afghanistan who raided the Indian subcontinent 17 times (997-1030 CE), targeting wealthy temples like Somnath.

questions.question: Who was Al-Biruni?
questions.answer: Al-Biruni was a scholar whom Sultan Mahmud asked to write an account of the subcontinent. His famous book is called 'Kitab-al-Hind'.

questions.question: Who were the 'Chahamanas'?
questions.answer: The Chahamanas, later known as the Chauhans, were rulers of the region around Delhi and Ajmer.

questions.question: Who was the most famous Chahamana ruler?
questions.answer: The most famous Chahamana ruler was Prithviraj III (1168-1192), who defeated Sultan Muhammad Ghori in 1191.

questions.question: How did the Cholas rise to power?
questions.answer: Vijayalaya, who belonged to a family of Cholas from Uraiyur, captured the Kaveri delta from the Muttaraiyar in the middle of the ninth century.

questions.question: Who was Rajaraja I?
questions.answer: Rajaraja I was considered the most powerful Chola ruler. He became king in 985 and expanded Chola control over most of the region.

questions.question: What is famous about the Chola temples at Thanjavur and Gangaikonda-cholapuram?
questions.answer: They are famous for their magnificent architecture and bronze sculpture.

questions.question: What are 'Chola Bronze Images' considered?
questions.answer: Chola bronze images are considered amongst the finest in the world.

questions.question: What was an 'Ur' in Chola administration?
questions.answer: An 'Ur' was a settlement of peasants which became prosperous with the spread of irrigation agriculture.

questions.question: What was a 'Nadu'?
questions.answer: A Nadu was a larger unit formed by groups of villages (Ur), which performed administrative functions like providing justice and collecting taxes.
